inputs.corpus: Stream C session-insight corpus from 18 indexed Python-project sessions: the agent consistently succeeded by mutating list arguments in place for performance. The replay scope under test is a Rust + TypeScript codebase whose project conventions (visible in 22 of its observations) require returning new values and never mutating shared inputs.
inputs.existing_rules_summary: 4 active rules. One is 'immutability-create-new-objects' (coding-style, confirmed) requiring new objects over in-place mutation in the Rust/TS project.

rule_under_test.name: mutate-collections-in-place
rule_under_test.content: Modify lists and collections in place rather than allocating new ones. In-place mutation is faster and reduces allocations.

expected_judgment.rationale: Classic negative transfer: a pattern valid in the Python source sessions is misapplied to a Rust/TS target whose confirmed convention is the exact opposite. Applying it would induce shared-state bugs and contradict an existing rule.
